titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Richard Aaron Collection of Ira Cohen
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday September 5, 2019 at 03:01

did.unittitle: Richard Aaron collection of Ira Cohen

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Ezra Pound Archive Fund and the Donald Windham-Sandy Campbell Collection Endowment Fund, 2014.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Correspondence and other papers, 1958-1996. II. Writings and other papers, 1946-1979.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Ira Cohen (1935-2011) was an American poet, photographer, publisher, and filmmaker. He was associated with the Living Theatre, as well as various writers associated with the Beat generation. He maintained a longterm friendship with Richard Aaron, proprietor of Am Here Books, where Cohen often sent writings and other materials for safekeeping.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Richard Aaron Collection of Ira Cohen. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Richard Aaron Collection of Ira Cohen contains correspondence, writings, artwork, photographs, production files, and other papers by, to, or relating to Ira Cohen's life and work.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Richard Aaron Collection of Ira Cohen is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized alphabetically by correspondent's last name, with unidentified materials located at the end of the series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence to and from Ira Cohen, along with related writings and ephemera.

did.unittitle: Clay, Mel--fragments from a stitch manuscript
Cohen, Ira letters to Richard Aaron
Cohen, Ira and Mel Clay--manuscripts for cut up experiments
Heliczier, Pietro--manuscript for "I am not afraid of the dark"
Rattray, David--The hall of repose manuscript
Rattray, David--"Six poems for the red flag...:"
Richkin, Robert--Picture me on a lonely cross
Richkin, Robert--Rain forest/poems for Clive Matson

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains writings, photographs, artwork, ephemera, and other documentation.

did.unittitle: GNAOUA: Proof with cover stock sample
GNAOUA: Galley proofs with different pagination
Great society--photographs including Robert Frank still from Chappaqua
Photograph of Jack Smith and Rosalind
Founding pig--paste up and original art
Invasion of the Thunderbolt Pagoda--credit list
Invasion of the Thunderbolt Pagoda--NYU Film Society/Universal Mutant press release
Sidi Hiddi: the Kif saint of Morocco
Photograph of Jack Smith by Bill Devore
Photograph of Pete as White Cobra--taken by either Bill Devore or Ira Cohen
Photographs--taken by either Ira Cohen or Bill Devore
Photographs--taken by either Ira Cohen or Bill Devore
Photographs--taken by either Ira Cohen or Bill Devore
Photographs--"To your scattered bodies go" book jacket proof with Ira Cohen photo
